Description: Partner at a16z speedrun for 3+ years; focuses on talent and early-stage founders
Action_For_Interview: Know his exact title and team before the interview

Description: Led recruiting at Riot Games; made 37 hires in year 1 when expectation was 2; promoted 4x
Action_For_Interview: Reference his Riot background to show you did research

Description: Received offers from Amazon Google Facebook Airbnb a16z Riot - has been on both sides of the table
Action_For_Interview: Shows he understands what great hiring looks like from candidate side

Description: 15 years in recruiting across agency startups and VC
Action_For_Interview: Signals deep expertise - don't try to teach him basics

Description: Has conducted approximately 30000 interviews throughout career
Action_For_Interview: He has seen every trick - be genuine not performative

Description: First job was at Quiznos - proud that non-elite path led to elite outcomes
Action_For_Interview: He resonates with non-obvious profiles and hustle narratives

Description: Grew up in backwater Orlando - not a Silicon Valley native; went to non-elite college
Action_For_Interview: Don't pretend to be someone you're not - he values the underdog

Description: Runs 40+ miles per week; averages 7:45 pace; has run 16+ half marathons in 4 months
Action_For_Interview: Mention fitness or discipline if it comes up naturally - he connects with it

Description: Uses Claude Code for vibe coding; built first hosted website; excited about AI tools
Action_For_Interview: He is genuinely excited about Claude - mentioning this could be a rapport builder

Description: 330k+ followers; posts every day before 7:30am; 100M+ impressions
Action_For_Interview: He values consistency and discipline in output

Description: First manager who gave feedback Jordan could receive without getting defensive; promoted him 4x at Riot
Action_For_Interview: Shows what he values in a manager - caring + critical = turbo fuel

Description: Boss at a16z speedrun; encouraged OLAD posting strategy; runs speedrun investing team
Action_For_Interview: Andrew Chen is his direct boss and a key figure

Description: Colleague on a16z speedrun talent team; also does hiring content on LinkedIn
Action_For_Interview: Know the full team if you can

Description: Colleague on a16z speedrun talent team; ex-Riot Games; often embarrassed by Jordan's sign photos
Action_For_Interview: The sign guy meme is self-aware - he knows he's cringe and owns it

Description: Speedrun has invested in 300+ early stage companies with $300M+ deployed
Action_For_Interview: Show you understand the scope of what he manages

Description: Connects job seekers to portfolio companies; placed hundreds of engineers designers PMs
Action_For_Interview: He runs the talent operation not just advises on it

Description: Fellowship for students and recent grads; 15 fellows selected from 10000+ applicants
Action_For_Interview: Highly competitive program he is actively marketing

Description: $20k equity-free grant + up to $250k investment; build full-time this summer
Action_For_Interview: He is very proud of this program - understand both tracks

Description: Placed at fast-growing portco in full-time founding engineer role (not internship)
Action_For_Interview: Key distinction - full time not internship

Description: Both tracks include time with Marc Andreessen and Andrew Chen
Action_For_Interview: The network access is a key selling point he emphasizes

Description: Jordan actively fights for applicants; believes hiring teams have psychological dysfunction dismissing them
Action_For_Interview: Do NOT say you only respond to sourced candidates - he will push back hard

Description: Google found 4 interviews reaches 86% confidence; each additional adds less than 1% utility
Action_For_Interview: He cites this specifically - shows his evidence-based approach to hiring

Description: More than 4 interviews signals: role clarity issues OR poor interview skills OR inmates run asylum OR warring silos
Action_For_Interview: If asked about your experience with long processes - use this framework

Description: Hiring decisions should NOT be consensus endeavors; hiring manager is ultimately accountable for outcomes
Action_For_Interview: He hired against 9 no votes once - it worked out; he owns his calls

Description: If you solicit interest you must get them excited; you cannot expect passion from someone you cold approached
Action_For_Interview: He thinks expecting passion from sourced candidates is dumb

Description: Can articulate what they like what they don't like what they want next and what they're good/bad at
Action_For_Interview: Most people haven't reflected on this - be ready to answer clearly

Description: Great candidates don't want to win a job they aren't a fit for; willing to share self-disqualifying info
Action_For_Interview: Don't try to win the interview - work WITH him to find the right fit

Description: Stay positive; don't denigrate past employers or colleagues; if not ready to be positive don't interview
Action_For_Interview: Do NOT trash any past employer or manager even if asked

Description: Read everything about the company; ask LLMs to prep; learn about people you'll interview with
Action_For_Interview: Show you researched him specifically - mention posts you found insightful

Description: Leads with desire to make impact on business; not benefits or titles or tech stack preferences
Action_For_Interview: Frame your motivations around business impact not personal gain

Description: 60-90 seconds max; treat it like a highlight reel not memoir; hit each role briefly; hobbies barely belong
Action_For_Interview: Prepare a tight 90-second version of your story

Description: Nothing lands better than seeing the business win; close second is watching team succeed; red flags: money titles
Action_For_Interview: Do NOT say money or titles even if that's part of it

Description: Don't answer this directly; pivot immediately to what you're running toward; any negativity about employer is BAD
Action_For_Interview: Prepare a forward-looking answer that doesn't mention negatives

Description: No number full stop; say you'd rather understand the complete opportunity first; this is negotiating leverage
Action_For_Interview: He teaches this publicly - he will respect you for following it

Description: Partner with manager and stakeholders to establish what matters; separate urgency from real impact; give concrete story
Action_For_Interview: Have a real story ready about something you consciously deprioritized

Entity: Where Do You See Yourself In 5 Years
Description: Loyalty test not career planning; talk about sharpening craft and expanding impact; wherever I can best serve business
Action_For_Interview: Wherever I can best serve the business lands every time per Jordan

Description: Don't sell yourself short; they're lucky to have you too; great employers see inquisitiveness as positive signal
Action_For_Interview: Prepare 3-5 genuinely sharp questions that show you evaluated the opportunity too

Description: Jordan recommends Claude specifically for story refinement and long-form resume work
Action_For_Interview: Literally using Claude Code to help you prep for this interview is on-brand

Description: Jordan recommends ChatGPT for mock interviews and rapid drill practice
Action_For_Interview: Mention you used AI tools to prepare - he thinks that's smart

Description: Jordan recommends Perplexity for real-time company research with live sources
Action_For_Interview: Show you used multiple tools to research a16z speedrun

Description: Jordan is impressed by Manus for contextual job searching with complex filters
Action_For_Interview: Emerging tool he's excited about - shows he keeps up with new AI tools

Description: Jordan went from no coding experience to building hosted projects in 2 weeks using Claude Code
Action_For_Interview: He finds vibe coding both exciting and terrifying for the future of work

Description: 2 in 3 candidates flagged for suspicious AI use in technical assessments; higher at elite universities
Action_For_Interview: He is watching for cheating - if assessed be authentic about tool use

Description: Some candidates use deepfake stand-ins in video interviews - he has encountered this
Action_For_Interview: Camera on mandatory in his view - camera off in interview is a no

Description: Skeptical of 12-month SWE full automation; believes 10 year change more realistic; cites Gates quote
Action_For_Interview: He is thoughtful here - not a doom-monger or a denier

Description: One Line A Day posting; must be published before 7:30am; has done this 600+ consecutive days
Action_For_Interview: This daily discipline mirrors his 'dailies' philosophy about work habits

Description: 43 of his top 50 posts had images; most had a human face; slightly illegible images create dwell time
Action_For_Interview: He values authenticity over polish in all areas

Description: Don't make things look perfect; say what you actually think; swallow your pride and be different
Action_For_Interview: Matching this energy in your interview will land better than being overly polished

Description: Humor posts drive engagement; engagement helps priming; value posts drive followers; they compound
Action_For_Interview: He has a systems-thinking approach to content - it's not random

Description: Changed one emoji in CTA and got 3x performance; heart-on-fire emoji dominates
Action_For_Interview: Fun detail to reference if you want to show you read his posts deeply

Description: Startup death does not equal career death; equity can be life-changing; you get more responsibility faster
Action_For_Interview: He believes in startups viscerally - his beach house came from equity

Description: Most startup jobs never touch LinkedIn - they hire via referral or talent network intros
Action_For_Interview: The core value prop of his talent network

Description: Use site:boards.greenhouse.io or site:ashbyhq.com with boolean search to find unlisted jobs
Action_For_Interview: He teaches this publicly - showing you know it signals you're resourceful

Description: Replace 86400 with 3600 in LinkedIn job URL + sort most recent = jobs posted in last hour
Action_For_Interview: Another signal of resourcefulness if you mention knowing this

Description: a16z; YC; Sequoia; Greylock; Bessemer; Kleiner Perkins all have portfolio job boards
Action_For_Interview: Know that the a16z job board is the one he most directly influences

Description: Remote does not mean live anywhere; tax nexus issues; labor laws vary; EOR doesn't solve everything
Action_For_Interview: He has nuanced views here - not blindly pro or anti remote

Description: Good hires typically take 2 months not 2 weeks; humans are not commodities ordered off a shelf
Action_For_Interview: If asked about your timeline - be honest and realistic

Description: Consistent daily rituals drive output; he does 50 outbound reachouts every morning before reading email
Action_For_Interview: He would respond well to you describing a consistent daily practice you have

Description: Best manager combo: cares about you + believes in you + gives deeply critical and earned feedback
Action_For_Interview: Eric Danker embodied this for him - show you can give and receive feedback

Description: Best employees see employer relationship as mutual not adversarial; clownfish and anemone analogy
Action_For_Interview: Don't show any cynicism about past employers - he reads it as dysfunction

Description: People protect ego by endless debate instead of shipping; ship 10 iterations before peers decide on one
Action_For_Interview: He respects action over deliberation - have examples of moving fast

Description: Warning against making bad decisions just because they sometimes work out; don't confuse luck with skill
Action_For_Interview: Shows he values clear probabilistic thinking over narrative reasoning

Description: Core traits are fixed after adulthood; growth is learning to manage not change core character
Action_For_Interview: He is self-aware about his own challenging traits - show similar self-awareness

Description: Luck plays a real role but capitalizing on lucky moments requires prior relationship building and great work
Action_For_Interview: His day-2 story at a16z - luck met preparation; have a similar story ready

Description: Camera off in a video interview that was communicated as video = no from him
Action_For_Interview: Do NOT do this in your interview with him

Description: No call no show = likely no reschedule; candidates canceling via Calendly mid-call tilts him more
Action_For_Interview: Be early to his interview and communicate if anything changes

Description: Any negativity about current employer in an interview is bad; stay positive and forward-looking
Action_For_Interview: Even if your last job was terrible frame your departure positively

Description: Never give a salary number first in a screening call; you burn your negotiating leverage
Action_For_Interview: If he asks defer gracefully

Description: Listing professional certs in education section signals concerted distraction from actual degree
Action_For_Interview: Don't front-load certs over substance - he notices

Description: Candidates saying they are actively recruiting (as job seekers) is a new trend he finds confusing
Action_For_Interview: Fun conversational detail if the interview gets casual

Description: Best cold emails are concise don't list accolades and don't call themselves executives
Action_For_Interview: He shared a specific example publicly - short and direct beats comprehensive

Description: Schedule interviews around 1pm - interviewers are happiest then; avoid 9am and 4:30pm
Action_For_Interview: If you have scheduling flexibility aim for midday

Description: Failed posts by definition won't be seen by many people; treat posting as daily requirement
Action_For_Interview: He has a growth mindset about putting things out into the world

Description: Goal is to find non-obvious people like Palmer Luckey (community college) or Marc Andreessen (rural Wisconsin)
Action_For_Interview: He actively looks past prestige signals - be genuine about your actual path

Description: Jordan cancelled $130k/yr LinkedIn Recruiter subscription for Juicebox at less than 10% the cost
Action_For_Interview: Shows he's cost-conscious and willing to disrupt established tools

Description: Act like they're lucky to have you; ask serious questions as the other party evaluating the fit
Action_For_Interview: Don't be desperate or overly deferential - show genuine mutual evaluation
